products.shortDescription: A traditional Sindhi blend of seasonal vegetables and fruit, pickled together in mustard oil and whole spice.
products.description: Our Mix Achar brings together mango, lemon, green chili, carrot and garlic in one jar — pickled the traditional Shikarpuri way in cold-pressed mustard oil with whole and ground spices. It's the pickle families keep on the table for every single meal, balancing heat, tang and depth in every spoonful.
products.seoTitle: Mix Achar (مکس اچار) 400g & 800g | Original Shikarpuri Achar Since 1985
products.seoDescription: Order authentic Shikarpuri Mix Achar online — traditional Sindhi mixed pickle in mustard oil. 400g & 800g jars, Cash on Delivery across Pakistan. Order on WhatsApp.
products.whatIsIt: Mix Achar is Shikarpur's everyday table pickle — a single jar of raw mango, green chili, carrot and garlic, all pickled together in cold-pressed mustard oil. It's the achar most Sindhi households keep open at every meal, because it covers several cravings in one spoonful.
products.traditionalBackground: In Shikarpur, mixed pickles developed out of necessity as much as taste — families pickled whatever vegetables and fruit were in season together, in one large matka (clay jar), rather than keeping separate jars for each item. Mustard oil was the natural preservative available locally, and combining several ingredients in a single achar became a Sindhi household signature, distinct from the single-ingredient pickles more common further north.
products.tasteProfileText: Because it blends several ingredients, Mix Achar changes character spoonful to spoonful — one bite leans into the mango's sourness, the next into raw green chili heat, the next into garlic's depth. Mustard oil ties it together with a sharp, slightly bitter backbone that mellows the longer the jar sits.
products.howToEat: Spoon a small amount alongside daal chawal, khichdi or plain roti — Mix Achar is built to be a side, not a main flavour, so a teaspoon per meal is typical. It's also a common addition to parathas, and many households stir a spoonful into plain yogurt raita for extra tang.
products.whoShouldBuy: If you only want to keep one achar jar in the kitchen, Mix Achar is the one to buy — it's the closest thing to a taste of everything the brand makes. It suits people who like variety in a single jar rather than committing to one dominant flavour.

products.shortDescription: Raw green mango pickle, sun-matured with mustard oil and Shikarpuri spice for a bold tangy bite.
products.description: Made from firm raw mangoes cut by hand and matured in mustard oil, our Aam Ka Achar is the pickle Shikarpur is famous for. Sharp, tangy and rounded out with fenugreek and mustard seed, it's a staple beside daal-chawal, parathas or plain roti.
products.seoTitle: Aam Ka Achar (آم کا اچار) 400g & 800g | Original Shikarpuri Achar Since 1985
products.seoDescription: Authentic Shikarpuri Aam Ka Achar — raw mango pickle in mustard oil, made the traditional Sindhi way since 1985. Order online with Cash on Delivery.
products.whatIsIt: Aam Ka Achar is our raw mango pickle — firm, unripe green mangoes hand-cut and matured in mustard oil with fenugreek, mustard seed and red chili. It's the pickle most people picture when they think of Shikarpuri achar.
products.traditionalBackground: Raw mango pickling is one of the oldest preservation methods in Sindh, tied to the mango harvest season when unripe fruit was picked in bulk before the summer heat and salted down to last the rest of the year. Shikarpur's version leans on fenugreek and mustard seed rather than the sweeter masalas used in some other regional styles, giving it a sharper, more sour-forward profile that's become the city's signature.
products.tasteProfileText: This is a sour-forward achar first, spice second — the raw mango's tartness dominates, with fenugreek adding a faint bitterness and mustard oil rounding out the sharpness. It doesn't mellow into sweetness the way ripe-mango preparations do; the tang stays firm even months after the jar is opened.
products.howToEat: The classic pairing is daal chawal or khichdi, where the sourness cuts through the blandness of plain rice and lentils. It's equally common alongside parathas at breakfast, or as a side with biryani to balance the rice's richness.
products.whoShouldBuy: Choose Aam Ka Achar if you specifically want the sour, traditional Shikarpuri flavour rather than a milder or sweeter achar — it's not for people who prefer sweet-and-sour styles (our Mango Sweet & Sour Achar is the milder option).

products.shortDescription: Gunda (lasora) pickle — a Sindhi classic, slow-pickled to a soft, tangy, spice-forward finish.
products.description: Lasoora (gunda) is a beloved Sindhi pickling fruit, and our version is prepared the old way — cleaned, salted and slow-matured in mustard oil with a warm spice blend until every piece is tender and packed with flavor.
products.seoTitle: Lasooray Ka Achar (لسوڑے کا اچار) | Original Shikarpuri Achar Since 1985
products.seoDescription: Traditional Sindhi Lasora (Gunda) pickle, slow-matured in mustard oil. 400g & 800g. Order online with Cash on Delivery across Pakistan.
products.whatIsIt: Lasooray Ka Achar is made from lasora (gunda) — a small, round fruit used specifically for pickling in South Asia — cleaned, salted and slow-matured in mustard oil with fennel and mustard seed until each piece turns soft and tangy.
products.traditionalBackground: Lasora is a fruit most people outside Sindh have never pickled, but it's a staple here — the trees grow across rural Sindh and the fruit is harvested specifically for pickling since it isn't commonly eaten fresh. Preparing lasora achar the old way, slow-matured rather than quick-pickled, is considered a mark of a serious achar-maker, since rushing the process leaves the fruit tough instead of tender.
products.tasteProfileText: Lasora has a naturally tender, slightly slippery texture once pickled — quite different from mango or chili achar — with a mild tang and fennel's faint sweetness running through the mustard-oil base. It's less aggressively spicy than most of our achar, letting the fruit's own character come through.
products.howToEat: Best eaten as a standalone side rather than mixed into a dish — a few pieces alongside daal chawal or roti let the texture and flavour stand on their own. It's a good introduction for anyone who's never tried lasora before.
products.whoShouldBuy: If you grew up with lasora achar and want the authentic version, or if you're curious to try a pickling fruit specific to Sindh rather than the more commonly available mango or lemon achar, this is worth trying.

products.shortDescription: Whole garlic cloves pickled until soft and mellow, finished with a warm, rich masala.
products.description: Whole garlic cloves are pickled slowly until the sharp edge mellows into a rich, savory depth. A favorite for those who love bold flavor, our Lehsan Ka Achar pairs perfectly with barbecue, biryani and everyday meals.
products.seoTitle: Lehsan Ka Achar (لہسن کا اچار) Garlic Pickle | ShikarpuriAchar.pk
products.seoDescription: Authentic whole garlic pickle (Lehsan Achar), pickled the traditional Shikarpuri way. 400g & 800g jars. Order on WhatsApp with COD.
products.whatIsIt: Lehsan Ka Achar is whole garlic cloves pickled slowly in mustard oil until the sharp raw edge mellows into a rich, savoury depth. Every clove stays whole — nothing is crushed or minced.
products.traditionalBackground: Garlic achar has long been valued in Sindhi households both as a condiment and for its believed digestive benefits, often kept on hand specifically for richer meals like biryani or barbecue. Because whole garlic takes longer to mature than most vegetables, a well-made jar of Lehsan Achar is often judged by how long it's been left to sit — the longer it matures, the milder and more savoury the cloves become.
products.tasteProfileText: Fresh garlic is sharp and biting; pickled garlic is a different thing entirely — savoury, rounded and only mildly pungent, with mustard oil and red chili adding warmth rather than raw heat. It's bold, but not sour or fiery the way chili or mango achar can be.
products.howToEat: A few cloves alongside biryani, barbecue or grilled meat is the classic pairing — the pickle cuts through richness the way a chutney would. It also works well with parathas or daal chawal for anyone who simply loves garlic.
products.whoShouldBuy: Best for people who already like garlic and want it in a bold, savoury, ready-to-eat form — not recommended if you're sensitive to strong garlic flavour, since whole-clove pickling doesn't dilute it much.

products.seoTitle: Adrak Ka Achar (ادرک کا اچار) Ginger Pickle | ShikarpuriAchar.pk
products.seoDescription: Traditional Shikarpuri ginger pickle (Adrak Achar) in mustard oil. 400g & 800g. Order online, Cash on Delivery across Pakistan.
products.whatIsIt: Adrak Ka Achar is fresh ginger, cut fine and pickled in mustard oil with a warm, aromatic spice blend — sharp, zesty and noticeably more warming than our other achar.
products.traditionalBackground: Ginger achar has a practical history in Sindhi households — it was traditionally kept close at hand during the monsoon and winter months, valued for cutting through heavy, oil-rich meals and for its warming effect. Unlike mango or lasora achar, which need months to mature, ginger achar is often ready sooner, making it a common quick-turnaround pickle in home kitchens.
products.tasteProfileText: The dominant note is ginger's own sharp heat, not chili heat — it warms rather than burns, with mustard seed and turmeric adding an earthy, slightly bitter undertone. It's more aromatic than most of our other achar, noticeably so as soon as the jar is opened.
products.howToEat: A small amount goes a long way — a few pieces alongside rich or fried food (paratha, fried fish, heavier curries) helps cut through the oiliness. It's also commonly eaten in small amounts on its own as a digestive after a heavy meal.
products.whoShouldBuy: Good for anyone who wants a warming, aromatic achar rather than a sour or fiery one, or who specifically enjoys ginger's flavour on its own terms.

products.description: Whole lemons are salt-cured and left to mature slowly, developing a deep, tangy flavor that's a staple on any Sindhi dastarkhwan. Our Lemon Ka Achar is bright, zesty and built to last.
products.seoTitle: Lemon Ka Achar (لیموں کا اچار) | Original Shikarpuri Achar Since 1985

products.whatIsIt: Lemon Ka Achar is whole lemons, salt-cured and left to mature slowly until they turn into a deep, tangy, almost jammy pickle — no cutting into small pieces, the lemons stay whole through the process.
products.traditionalBackground: Salt-curing whole lemons rather than cutting them is the older, slower method — it takes longer for the rind to soften and the flavour to develop, but it produces a rounder, less harshly sour result than quick-pickled lemon. This whole-lemon method has stayed the standard in Shikarpuri kitchens even as faster alternatives became available elsewhere.
products.tasteProfileText: Salt-cured lemon develops a flavour that's tangy but also slightly bitter from the rind and mellowed by the long cure — different from fresh lemon juice, closer to a concentrated, preserved citrus flavour. Fenugreek and red chili round it out without overpowering the citrus.
products.howToEat: Traditionally served in small quantities alongside daal chawal or biryani, where its acidity brightens a heavier dish. Some households also chop a piece finely into raita or chutney for extra tang.
products.whoShouldBuy: Choose this if you want a citrus-forward achar rather than a mango, garlic or chili-based one — a good pick for people who prefer their food with a bright, sour edge.

products.tasteProfileText: Unlike the soft texture of mango or lasora achar, Pyaz Ka Achar stays crunchy — the onion's natural sweetness balances against the tang of mustard oil and vinegar, with red chili adding a light kick rather than dominant heat.
